command_examples: sqld  SQL Direct (directly inserts into MySQL/PostgreSQL database)

usage_sections: BODY: and !BODY: — Match or exclude content in the response body.
HEADER: and !HEADER: — Match or exclude content in HTTP headers.
COOKIE: and !COOKIE: — Match or exclude content in HTTP cookies. (NEW)
CODE: and !CODE: — Match or exclude HTTP status codes.

arguments.name: -Add-header
arguments.description: Add HTTP headers (can be used multiple times, one per header pair)

arguments.name: -check6
arguments.description: Check if IPv6 is working (connects to ipv6.google.com or value set in nikto.conf)

arguments.name: -dbcheck
arguments.description: Check database and other key files for syntax errors

arguments.name: -list-plugins
arguments.description: List all available plugins, perform no testing

arguments.name: -no404
arguments.description: Disables nikto attempting to guess a 404 page

arguments.name: -nocheck
arguments.description: Don't check for updates on startup

arguments.name: -nocookies
arguments.description: Do not use cookies from responses in requests (cookies are stored and sent by default)

arguments.name: -noslash
arguments.description: Strip trailing slash from URL (e.g., '/admin/' to '/admin')

arguments.name: -Option
arguments.description: Over-ride an option in nikto.conf, can be issued multiple times

arguments.name: -Save
arguments.description: Save positive responses to this directory ('.' for auto-name)

arguments.name: -useproxy
arguments.description: Use the proxy defined in nikto.conf, or argument http://server:port

arguments.name: -useragent
arguments.description: Force User-Agent instead of pulling from database

arguments.name: -Userdbs
arguments.description: Load only user databases, not the standard databases

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

examples: sqld  SQL Direct (directly inserts into MySQL/PostgreSQL database)

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zodiacon/HookHunter (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zmap/zmap (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: Nikto web server scanner - https://cirt.net/Nikto2.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Nikto web server scanner - https://cirt.net/Nikto2.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/sullo/nikto; git clone https://github.com/sullo/nikto.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sqld  SQL Direct (directly inserts into MySQL/PostgreSQL database)`. Further invocations from upstream: `Nikto web server scanner  - $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Nikto web server scanner - https://cirt.net/Nikto2.

name: nikto

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need web, use nikto because Nikto web server scanner - https://cirt.net/Nikto2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sqld  SQL Direct (directly inserts into MySQL/PostgreSQL database)` as documented upstream for nikto.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Nikto web server scanner  - $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for nikto.
When chaining web tools, pair nikto with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `nikto --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
